one_line_takeaway: Machine unlearning can be achieved by a single universal input perturbation with model weights frozen, matching weight-editing methods on forgetting effectiveness (and MIA resistance) at the cost of somewhat lower retained-model utility, with class-wise 'forget vectors' composable via arithmetic to serve new deletion requests.
key_finding: An input-space perturbation with weights held fixed can match model-based approximate unlearning. On class-wise forgetting (CIFAR-10, ResNet-18) the forget vector reaches Avg. Gap 2.30 vs Retrain (best-in-class MIA-Efficacy 99.60, closest to Retrain's 100), essentially tying the best weight-editing method SalUn (2.19) and beating SCRUB (2.64) and SSD (2.70). On random data forgetting (CIFAR-10, ResNet-18) it gives the lowest Avg. Gap of all methods, 2.92. On ImageNet-10 with ViT it reaches Avg. Gap 1.53 (class-wise) and 0.69 (random). The consistent trade-off: the forget vector matches or beats baselines on unlearning EFFECTIVENESS (UA, MIA-Efficacy) but tends to lose slightly more model UTILITY (RA, TA drop, e.g. TA 90.90 vs Retrain 94.92 on CIFAR-10 class-wise), since perturbing inputs also perturbs retained/test data. Compositional unlearning (CU-FV) reaches Avg. Gap close to a directly-trained forget vector (e.g. 3.16 vs 2.92 on CIFAR-10 random) while optimizing far fewer parameters, and the forget vector transfers to unseen forget sets (UA 98.26 on held-out test data of the forgotten class, Table 2).
core_contribution: The paper proposes the 'forget vector': a single, input-agnostic (data-agnostic) additive perturbation delta that, when added to inputs targeted for deletion, induces machine unlearning in an image classifier WITHOUT modifying any model weights. This inverts the standard machine-unlearning (MU) paradigm, which is almost entirely model-based (retraining/fine-tuning/gradient-ascent on weights). The authors' own contributions are: (1) an OOD-generalization sensitivity study showing existing model-based unlearning stays effective under forget-data shifts (Gaussian noise, elastic transforms, PGD) while utility degrades; (2) optimizing a universal forget vector via a C&W-style margin loss plus a retain-set cross-entropy regularizer and an l2 penalty; and (3) 'forget vector arithmetic' / compositional unlearning (CU-FV), where precomputed class-wise forget vectors are linearly combined (delta(w)=sum w_k delta_k) to handle new random-subset forgetting requests by optimizing only the combination coefficients w, not a full new vector. Note: visual prompting, model reprogramming, and task-vector arithmetic (Ilharco et al.) are cited inspiration/Related Work, NOT this paper's own inventions; the novelty is applying an input-space perturbation to the unlearning objective and doing input-based (rather than weight-space) task-vector arithmetic.
threat_model: This is a privacy/model-editing paper (machine unlearning for the 'right to be forgotten'), so it sits in the security/privacy area, but it is NOT an attack/defense paper and defines no adversary as its main object. The 'unlearner' is a benign model owner who wants to erase the influence of a forget set Df while preserving utility on the retain set Dr. Adversarial techniques (C&W margin loss, PGD) are borrowed as OPTIMIZATION and evaluation tools, not threats. The paper explicitly states it does NOT consider counter-unlearning adversaries who would try to negate the forget vector's effect. Membership inference attack (MIA-Efficacy) is used only as an evaluation metric for unlearning success, not as a modeled attack surface. The authors themselves flag as a limitation that the forget vector may not be robust to white-box adversaries aware of the forget-vector strategy.
limitations: Author-acknowledged: (1) the forget vector may not be robust to white-box adversaries who know the forget-vector strategy; (2) compositional unlearning depends on precomputed class-wise forget vectors, which can be costly to obtain. Reviewer-observed: (3) evaluation asymmetry — the forget vector is scored on perturbed evaluation sets while all model-based baselines are scored on clean sets; the paper argues this is fair (same forgetting objective) but it complicates head-to-head comparison. (4) Utility retention (RA/TA) is systematically weaker than weight-editing methods because the same perturbation contaminates retain/test inputs. (5) Scale is modest: only CIFAR-10 and a 10-class ImageNet subset (ImageNet-10), chosen so Retrain remains feasible; no full ImageNet or large-model results. (6) No formal privacy/removal guarantee — this is approximate, empirically-measured unlearning, and compositional unlearning lacks theoretical grounding (noted as future work). (7) The method requires applying delta at inference to every input flagged for deletion, i.e. unlearning lives in a deployment-time input pipeline rather than in the model itself.

datasets_used: ImageNet-10 (a 10-class subset of ImageNet, used so exact Retrain is feasible)
quantitative_results: Class-wise forgetting, CIFAR-10/ResNet-18: Ours (forget vector) UA 97.88, MIA-Efficacy 99.60, RA 97.25, TA 90.90, Avg. Gap 2.30 (vs Retrain 100/100/99.91/94.92)
Class-wise forgetting, CIFAR-10/ResNet-18 baselines' Avg. Gap: SalUn 2.19, SCRUB 2.64, SSD 2.70, GA 12.68, RL 20.67, FT 35.86
Random data forgetting (10%), CIFAR-10/ResNet-18: Ours Avg. Gap 2.92 (lowest of all methods; SalUn 3.23, SCRUB 3.31, NegGrad+ 3.67, SSD 3.70, GA 3.81, FT 4.15)
Class-wise forgetting, ImageNet-10/ViT: Ours Avg. Gap 1.53 (SCRUB 1.71, RL 3.84, SalUn 4.08, SSD 13.14)
Random data forgetting, ImageNet-10/ViT: Ours Avg. Gap 0.69 (SSD 0.28 best, SCRUB 0.87, FT 0.89)
Transferability (Table 2, UA %, CIFAR-10/ResNet-18): forget vector reaches UA 98.26 on held-out test data of forgotten class; 78.32 on GN1-perturbed forget set; 85.03 on ET1-perturbed forget set

Compositional unlearning (Table 3, CIFAR-10/ResNet-18 random forgetting): CU-FV Avg. Gap 3.16 vs directly-trained FV 2.92; ImageNet-10/VGG-16: CU-FV 2.47 vs FV 0.99; ImageNet-10/ViT: CU-FV 0.75 vs FV 0.69
Forget-vector arithmetic sweep (Fig. 3): best two-class combination on CIFAR-10 found at w1=-0.1, w2=0.1; moderate weights (|w|<=0.1) balance UA-gap vs RA-gap
Hyperparameters: margin tau=1; lambda1 in {3 CIFAR-10, 5 ImageNet-10/VGG-16, 7 ImageNet-10/ViT} class-wise and 1 for random; lambda2=1; SGD momentum 0.9, batch 256, max 40 iterations, 10 trials
baselines_compared: SalUn (gradient-based weight saliency, Fan et al. 2023)
Class-F (Kodge et al. 2024; class-wise forgetting only)
SSD (Selective Synaptic Dampening, Foster et al. 2024a)
